scenario: Halcyon Marketplace plans 12 process tests against a Camunda 8.8 invoice approval flow, distributed across the happy path, rejection, approval timeout, and archive error. The architect wants a clear definition of the chunk-based strategy before writing the first deviation test.
question: What does testing a Camunda 8.8 process in chunks actually mean according to the best practices?

options.text: Identify the important paths and chunks of the process, such as happy path, rejection, approval timeout, and archive error, and write a dedicated test method for each chunk so deviations are covered alongside the happy path
Slice the BPMN file into smaller BPMN files at every gateway and write a test for each slice separately, so the original process model is only assembled when running integration tests
Pick a single representative process instance per release and use it as the only chunk under test, on the assumption that other paths will be exercised manually during exploratory testing
Cover only error paths with dedicated test methods; the happy path is sufficiently covered by the production telemetry produced by Operate during the first week after a release

a.text: Correct. The Testing your process in chunks section recommends identifying the important paths of the process (happy path, rejection, timer-driven automatic approval, archive error) and dedicating a test method to each chunk, with the happy path tested fully in one big method.

b.text: Incorrect. The best practice does not split the BPMN file itself; it splits the test coverage across important paths through the same model.

c.text: Incorrect. One chunk per release leaves the deviating paths uncovered, which is the opposite of the chunk-based recommendation.

d.text: Incorrect. The happy path is part of the chunk-based strategy and is explicitly tested in one consistent end-to-end method, not deferred to Operate.

explanation: Testing a Camunda 8.8 process in chunks means writing dedicated test methods for the important paths through the model, such as the happy path plus rejection, timer-driven approval, and archive error.
